content:
# Tasks Breakdown: Hybrid Email Attachment System ## Overview This document breaks down the implementation of the hybrid email attachment system into atomic, testable tasks. Each task is designed to be completed independently with clear acceptance criteria and test requirements. --- ## Task Categories ### 1. Core Services (Backend) ### 2. Email Composition (Backend) ### 3. Testing & Quality ### 4. Documentation --- ## 1. Core Services (Backend) ### Task 1.1: Create AttachmentProcessorService **File:** `backend/src/modules/email/services/attachment-processor.service.ts` **Description:** Create new service for processing attachments with size-based decision logic. **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Service created with proper NestJS `@Injectable()` decorator - [ ] Define interfaces: `ProcessedAttachment`, `DriveLink`, `ProcessedAttachments` - [ ] Implement `processAttachments()` method with size threshold logic (5MB) - [ ] Files sorted by size (smallest first) before processing - [ ] Total size validation (20MB Gmail safety limit) - [ ] Download failure fallback to Drive links (no exceptions thrown) - [ ] Proper logging for all decision points - [ ] Inject `GoogleDriveClient` and `Logger` dependencies **Implementation Notes:** ```typescript // Key constants private readonly SIZE_THRESHOLD_MB = 5; private readonly GMAIL_SAFE_LIMIT_MB = 20; // Decision logic order: // 1. Check file size >= 5MB → link // 2. Check total size would exceed 20MB → link // 3. Attempt download → on success: attachment, on failure: link ``` **Files to Create:** - `backend/src/modules/email/services/attachment-processor.service.ts` **Dependencies:** - `GoogleDriveClient` (existing) - `Logger` (NestJS) --- ### Task 1.2: Add downloadFile() to GoogleDriveClient **File:** `backend/src/modules/attachments/services/google-drive-client.service.ts` **Description:** Add method to download file from Google Drive as in-memory Buffer. **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Method signature: `async downloadFile(userId: string, fileId: string): Promise` - [ ] Use `alt: 'media'` parameter to get file content (not metadata) - [ ] Use `responseType: 'arraybuffer'` for binary data - [ ] Return Buffer from ArrayBuffer - [ ] Reuse existing `retryOperation()` for reliability (3 retries, exponential backoff) - [ ] Reuse existing `handleDriveError()` for error handling - [ ] Reuse existing `getDriveClient()` for OAuth token management - [ ] Log success/failure with fileId **Implementation Notes:** ```typescript const response = await drive.files.get( { fileId, alt: 'media' }, { responseType: 'arraybuffer' } ); return Buffer.from(response.data as ArrayBuffer); ``` **Files to Modify:** - `backend/src/modules/attachments/services/google-drive-client.service.ts` **Dependencies:** - googleapis `drive_v3.Drive` (existing) - Existing retry/error handling methods --- ## 2. Email Composition (Backend) ### Task 2.1: Update GmailClient for multipart MIME **File:** `backend/src/modules/email/services/gmail-client.service.ts` **Description:** Add support for RFC 2822 multipart/mixed messages with attachments. **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Update `IEmailSendRequest` interface to include optional `attachments` array - [ ] Keep existing `createEmailMessage()` signature (backward compatible) - [ ] Extract current logic to `createSimpleMessage()` private method - [ ] Create new `createMultipartMessage()` private method - [ ] Decision logic: if attachments.length === 0, use simple format; else use multipart - [ ] Multipart format uses unique boundary delimiter - [ ] Each attachment encoded as base64 with proper MIME headers - [ ] Final message encoded as base64url for Gmail API **Implementation Notes:** ```typescript interface IEmailSendRequest { to: string; subject: string; body: string; isHtml?: boolean; attachments?: Array<{ // NEW filename: string; content: Buffer; mimeType: string; }>; } ``` **Multipart Structure:** ``` Content-Type: multipart/mixed; boundary="boundary_xxx" --boundary_xxx Content-Type: text/html; charset=utf-8 --boundary_xxx Content-Type: application/pdf Content-Disposition: attachment; filename="file.pdf" Content-Transfer-Encoding: base64 --boundary_xxx-- ``` **Files to Modify:** - `backend/src/modules/email/services/gmail-client.service.ts` - `packages/types/src/email/email.types.ts` (interface update) **Dependencies:** - None (pure message formatting) --- ### Task 2.2: Update EmailTemplateService for mixed rendering **File:** `backend/src/modules/email/services/email-template.service.ts` **Description:** Update template generation to render both attachments and Drive links. **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Add overload signature for `generateClaimEmail()` accepting `processedAttachments?: ProcessedAttachments` - [ ] Maintain backward compatibility (existing signature still works) - [ ] Update `generateAttachmentsContent()` to accept optional `ProcessedAttachments` - [ ] Render two sections: "📎 Attached Files" and "☁️ Files on Google Drive" - [ ] Show attachment count and file sizes - [ ] Add helper method `formatFileSize(bytes: number): string` - [ ] Maintain existing XSS escaping for all user input - [ ] Fallback to current behavior if `processedAttachments` is undefined **HTML Template Structure:** ```html

📎 Attached Files (N)

The following files are attached to this email:

  • filename.pdf (2.3 MB)

☁️ Files on Google Drive (N)

Click the links below to access larger files:

  • filename.mp4 (15.2 MB)
``` **Files to Modify:** - `backend/src/modules/email/services/email-template.service.ts` **Dependencies:** - `ProcessedAttachments` type from `AttachmentProcessorService` --- ### Task 2.3: Update EmailService orchestration **File:** `backend/src/modules/email/services/email.service.ts` **Description:** Integrate `AttachmentProcessorService` into email sending workflow. **Acceptance Criteria:** - [ ] Inject `AttachmentProcessorService` in constructor - [ ] Call `processAttachments()` after claim validation, before email generation - [ ] Pass `processedAttachments` to `generateClaimEmail()` - [ ] Map `processedAttachments.attachments` to Gmail API format - [ ] Pass attachments array to `gmailClient.sendEmail()` - [ ] Maintain existing error handling and transaction logic (unchanged) - [ ] Log attachment processing results **Implementation Changes:** ```typescript // NEW Step 2: Process attachments const processedAttachments = await this.attachmentProcessorService.processAttachments( userId, attachments, ); // MODIFIED Step 3: Pass processed attachments to template const emailContent = this.emailTemplateService.generateClaimEmail( claim!, user!, attachments, processedAttachments, ); // MODIFIED Step 4: Send email with attachments const emailResult = await this.gmailClient.sendEmail(userId, { to: emailRecipients, subject: emailSubject, body: emailContent, isHtml: true, attachments: processedAttachments.attachments.map(att => ({ filename: att.filename, content: att.buffer, mimeType: att.mimeType, })), }); ``` **Files to Modify:** - `backend/src/modules/email/services/email.service.ts` - `backend/src/modules/email/email.module.ts` (add `AttachmentProcessorService` to providers) **Dependencies:** - `AttachmentProcessorService` (Task 1.1) --- ## 3. Testing & Quality ### Task 3.1: Unit tests for AttachmentProcessorService **File:** `backend/src/modules/email/services/__tests__/attachment-processor.service.spec.ts` **Description:** Comprehensive unit tests for attachment processing logic. **Test Cases:** - [ ] Should attach files < 5MB - [ ] Should link files >= 5MB - [ ] Should fallback to link on download failure - [ ] Should respect 20MB total size limit - [ ] Should process smallest files first (sorting) - [ ] Should handle empty attachments array - [ ] Should handle missing googleDriveFileId (defensive) - [ ] Should log all decision points - [ ] Should accumulate total attachment size correctly **Mock Strategy:** - Mock `GoogleDriveClient.downloadFile()` to return Buffer or throw error - Mock `Logger` to verify logging calls **Files to Create:** - `backend/src/modules/email/services/__tests__/attachment-processor.service.spec.ts` **Coverage Target:** 100% line coverage --- ### Task 3.2: Unit tests for GoogleDriveClient.downloadFile **File:** `backend/src/modules/attachments/services/__tests__/google-drive-client.service.spec.ts` **Description:** Unit tests for new download method. **Test Cases:** - [ ] Should download file as Buffer - [ ] Should use alt='media' parameter - [ ] Should use responseType='arraybuffer' - [ ] Should retry on transient errors (503) - [ ] Should not retry on permanent errors (404) - [ ] Should throw on auth errors (401) - [ ] Should respect maxRetries limit (3 attempts) - [ ] Should use exponential backoff delays **Mock Strategy:** - Mock `drive.files.get()` from googleapis - Spy on `retryOperation()` to verify retry behavior **Files to Modify:** - `backend/src/modules/attachments/services/__tests__/google-drive-client.service.spec.ts` (add new tests) **Coverage Target:** 100% for new method --- ### Task 3.3: Unit tests for GmailClient multipart MIME **File:** `backend/src/modules/email/services/__tests__/gmail-client.service.spec.ts` **Description:** Unit tests for multipart message creation. **Test Cases:** - [ ] Should create simple message when no attachments - [ ] Should create multipart message with attachments - [ ] Should include proper MIME boundaries - [ ] Should encode attachments as base64 - [ ] Should include Content-Disposition headers - [ ] Should handle multiple attachments - [ ] Should encode final message as base64url - [ ] Should maintain backward compatibility **Mock Strategy:** - Use private method access for testing `createMultipartMessage()` - Decode base64url output to verify structure **Files to Modify:** - `backend/src/modules/email/services/__tests__/gmail-client.service.spec.ts` (add new tests) **Coverage Target:** 100% for new/modified code --- ### Task 3.4: Unit tests for EmailTemplateService mixed rendering **File:** `backend/src/modules/email/services/__tests__/email-template.service.spec.ts` **Description:** Unit tests for template generation with mixed attachments. **Test Cases:** - [ ] Should render attachments section when present - [ ] Should render links section when present - [ ] Should render both sections when mixed - [ ] Should show correct counts - [ ] Should format file sizes correctly (B, KB, MB) - [ ] Should escape HTML in filenames - [ ] Should fallback to current behavior when processedAttachments undefined - [ ] Should handle empty attachments and links **Mock Strategy:** - Create mock `ProcessedAttachments` objects - Parse HTML output to verify structure **Files to Modify:** - `backend/src/modules/email/services/__tests__/email-template.service.spec.ts` (add new tests) **Coverage Target:** 100% for new/modified code --- ### Task 3.5: Integration tests for email sending **File:** `backend/src/modules/email/services/__tests__/email.service.integration.spec.ts` **Description:** End-to-end integration tests for complete email workflow. **Test Cases:** - [ ] Should send email with mixed attachments and links - [ ] Should send email with attachments only - [ ] Should send email with links only - [ ] Should fallback all files to links on Drive API failure - [ ] Should update claim status to SENT on success - [ ] Should update claim status to FAILED on Gmail error - [ ] Should handle total size exceeding 20MB gracefully **Setup Strategy:** - Use real database with transactions - Mock external APIs (Gmail, Drive) - Create test claims with real attachments **Files to Create:** - `backend/src/modules/email/services/__tests__/email.service.integration.spec.ts` **Coverage Target:** All critical paths tested --- ### Task 3.6: Add formatFileSize helper tests **File:** `backend/src/modules/email/services/__tests__/email-template.service.spec.ts` **Description:** Unit tests for file size formatting utility. **Test Cases:** - [ ] Should format bytes (< 1KB) as "123 B" - [ ] Should format kilobytes (< 1MB) as "12.5 KB" - [ ] Should format megabytes as "5.2 MB" - [ ] Should handle 0 bytes - [ ] Should handle large files (> 100MB) - [ ] Should round to 1 decimal place **Files to Modify:** - `backend/src/modules/email/services/__tests__/email-template.service.spec.ts` **Coverage Target:** 100% --- ## 4. Documentation ### Task 4.1: Update API documentation **Files:** - `docs/project-info/api-endpoints.md` **Description:** Document changes to email send endpoint. **Updates Required:** - [ ] Document new behavior: hybrid attachment approach - [ ] Update request/response examples showing attachments - [ ] Document error codes for attachment processing failures - [ ] Add performance notes (latency may increase with downloads) --- ### Task 4.2: Update architecture documentation **Files:** - `docs/project-info/architecture.md` **Description:** Document new service and data flow. **Updates Required:** - [ ] Add `AttachmentProcessorService` to component diagram - [ ] Update data flow diagram showing attachment processing - [ ] Document size thresholds (5MB, 20MB) - [ ] Document fallback behavior --- ### Task 4.3: Add ADR (Architecture Decision Record) **File:** `docs/adr/003-hybrid-email-attachments.md` **Description:** Document architectural decision for hybrid approach. **Sections:** - [ ] Context: Why hybrid approach was chosen - [ ] Decision: Size-based threshold (5MB) - [ ] Consequences: Performance, reliability, complexity tradeoffs - [ ] Alternatives considered: Drive-only, attachment-only --- ## Task Execution Order ### Phase 1: Core Implementation (Tasks 1.1, 1.2) **Estimated Time:** 1 day ``` Task 1.2 → Task 1.1 (1.1 depends on 1.2) ``` **Rationale:** Implement download capability first, then build processor on top. --- ### Phase 2: Email Composition (Tasks 2.1, 2.2, 2.3) **Estimated Time:** 1 day ``` Task 2.1 → Task 2.2 → Task 2.3 ``` **Rationale:** Bottom-up integration - MIME formatting → template rendering → orchestration. --- ### Phase 3: Testing (Tasks 3.1-3.6) **Estimated Time:** 1.5 days ``` Parallel execution: - Task 3.1 (AttachmentProcessor tests) - Task 3.2 (GoogleDrive tests) - Task 3.3 (GmailClient tests) - Task 3.4 (EmailTemplate tests) - Task 3.6 (Helper tests) Then: - Task 3.5 (Integration tests - requires all above) ``` **Rationale:** Unit tests can run in parallel. Integration tests require all components. --- ### Phase 4: Documentation (Tasks 4.1-4.3) **Estimated Time:** 0.5 day ``` Parallel execution: - Task 4.1 (API docs) - Task 4.2 (Architecture docs) - Task 4.3 (ADR) ``` **Rationale:** Documentation tasks are independent. --- ## Total Estimated Time - Phase 1: 1 day - Phase 2: 1 day - Phase 3: 1.5 days - Phase 4: 0.5 day **Total: 4 days** (single developer, full-time) --- ## Definition of Done Each task is considered complete when: 1. **Code Implementation:** - [ ] All acceptance criteria met - [ ] Code follows project conventions (NestJS patterns, TypeScript strict mode) - [ ] All files created/modified as specified - [ ] No linting errors (`make lint` passes) - [ ] Code formatted (`make format` applied) 2. **Testing:** - [ ] Unit tests written with target coverage - [ ] All tests passing (`make test/unit` passes) - [ ] Integration tests passing (if applicable) 3. **Documentation:** - [ ] Inline code comments for complex logic - [ ] JSDoc comments for public methods - [ ] Related documentation updated 4. **Code Review:** - [ ] Self-review completed - [ ] No obvious bugs or edge cases missed --- ## Risk Mitigation ### High-Risk Tasks **Task 1.2 (GoogleDriveClient.downloadFile):** - **Risk:** Download failures causing email send failures - **Mitigation:** Comprehensive error handling with fallback to Drive links - **Validation:** Test with intentional API failures in Task 3.2 **Task 2.1 (GmailClient multipart MIME):** - **Risk:** Malformed MIME breaking email delivery - **Mitigation:** Follow RFC 2822 specification exactly, validate with Gmail API - **Validation:** Test with various attachment types/sizes in Task 3.3 **Task 3.5 (Integration tests):** - **Risk:** Missing edge cases causing production failures - **Mitigation:** Test all failure scenarios (size limits, API errors, mixed delivery) - **Validation:** Code review focusing on failure paths --- ## Dependencies Between Tasks ``` Task 1.2 (GoogleDrive.downloadFile) ↓ Task 1.1 (AttachmentProcessor) ──→ Task 3.1 (Processor tests) ↓ ↓ Task 2.3 (EmailService) ↓ ↑ ↓ Task 2.2 (EmailTemplate) ──→ Task 3.4 (Template tests) ↑ ↓ Task 2.1 (GmailClient) ──→ Task 3.3 (Gmail tests) ↓ Task 3.5 (Integration) ``` **Critical Path:** 1.2 → 1.1 → 2.3 → 3.5 --- ## Task Status Tracking | Task | Status | Assignee | Start Date | Completion Date | Notes | |------|--------|----------|------------|-----------------|-------| | 1.1 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | | 1.2 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | | 2.1 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | | 2.2 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | | 2.3 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | | 3.1 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | | 3.2 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | | 3.3 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | | 3.4 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | | 3.5 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | | 3.6 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | | 4.1 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | | 4.2 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | | 4.3 | [ ] Pending | - | - | - | - | --- **Document Version:** 1.0 **Last Updated:** 2025-09-30 **Status:** Ready for Implementation
